games.analysis: Tampa Bay's pitching depth and home field advantage make them slight favorites despite Miami's recent competitive efforts. The 64.3% confidence with +15.8% edge suggests modest but real value in backing the Rays at -148.
Atlanta's strong offensive lineup and pitching matchup favor them heavily, with the model showing solid 69.5% confidence and +14.5% edge. This is a high-conviction play on the Braves' home dominance.

The model shows Philadelphia as undervalued at +110 with 48.5% confidence but exceptional +18.6% edge—this is a classic positive-expectation underdog spot. The Pirates' recent struggles make this an attractive contrarian play.

Cleveland's 76.1% confidence is the highest on the slate, but the +6.0% edge is concerning—this suggests the market may have already priced in most of the Guardians' advantage. Proceed with caution despite high confidence.
Model conflict indicates statistical uncertainty between these AL Central rivals; the 6/10 chaos factor suggests lineup-dependent variance that models struggle to capture.

2-team.note: Pairing the two highest-confidence plays from the slate creates a parlay with combined confidence around 74-75% and eliminates chaos factor exposure. Both teams are home favorites with strong offensive matchups, reducing correlation risk while maintaining edge accumulation.
